scope.summary: Keeps the D8 rig doctor shellcheck-clean and verifies the local environment preflight refuses and passes through the script's own checks.
out_of_scope: Changing the L1 rig lifecycle command itself.
Running a full workspace Cargo gate.
Changing any sibling driver repository or path-overriding the published driver dependency.

entry_points.name: rig doctor shell lint
entry_points.trace: `shellcheck scripts/rig/rig_doctor.sh` exited 0 after the D8 shellcheck-clean repair.

entry_points.name: rig doctor anti-vacuity selftest
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/rig/rig_doctor.sh --selftest` exited 0 and printed: rig_doctor selftest: OK (every check can refuse; coverage matches /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh).

entry_points.name: local rig environment preflight
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/rig/rig_doctor.sh --lane all` exited 0 and reported PASS rig_doctor: 16 checks, 0 refusals across docker, timeout, state-dir, xe18, xe21, and free23 container/sqlplus checks.

limitations: This close proves the preflight against currently running local containers; it does not run the full L1 Oracle harness.
The full workspace Cargo gate was not run because this is a shell-only rig doctor repair and workspace-wide cargo is build-lease gated.
The shared checkout contains dirty files outside this bead's scope, so only the source-scoped tree cleanliness is claimed.
